allies.rationale_text: Decomposition helps cognitive-load-theory break a problem into logical pieces that working memory can actually manage.
allies.source_quote: Logic trees make it easy to see the structure of the problem. This limits the number of variables the working memory must consider simultaneously.

allies.rationale_text: Habit-formation frees working memory by automating routine tasks so cognitive effort can shift to the genuinely new challenge.
allies.source_quote: Habits reduce the cognitive load necessary for routine tasks, allowing the conscious mind to conserve effort for higher-level problems.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cognitive-load-theory conflicts with first-principles-thinking when deep decomposition overloads working memory before the core abstraction is stable.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cognitive-load-theory vs first-principles-thinking: cognitive-load-theory conflicts with first-principles-thinking (first principles thinking) when deep decomposition overloads working memory before core abstractions stabilize.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Cognitive-load-theory conflicts with optionality when too many parallel branches exceed practical decision bandwidth.
structured_tensions.source_quote: cognitive-load-theory vs optionality: cognitive-load-theory conflicts with optionality (optionality) when too many parallel branches exceed practical decision bandwidth.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on ally structure and explicit tensions, so the file stays sparse rather than forcing weak antagonist mappings.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation sprawl where the raw canonical id surface is weaker than the explicit ally and tension blocks.
open_questions: Curse-of-knowledge and boiling-the-ocean are source-real antagonists, but the current canonical target surface does not expose clean direct ids for them.
